title: Steel Demand To Remain Strong Amid The Economic Recovery
section_path: Steel Demand To Remain Strong Amid The Economic Recovery
section_path_text: Steel Demand To Remain Strong Amid The Economic Recovery
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
The global economic recovery is gaining momentum, with the US reporting that its economy expanded by 6.4 per cent on an annualized basis in the first quarter. While the US growth did not match the record-breaking pace of expansion in China, the rise allowed the real GDP to recuperate 91 per cent of the pandemic-induced drop. A strong recovery in consumer spending contributed to the robust gains, as jobless rates edged down and two sets of federal stimulus packages sent growth in personal consumption to the highest levels since the 1960s.
